summary: Cathy O'Neil announces that the final physical copy of her book 'Weapons of Math Destruction' has arrived, sharing photos of her editor and son holding it. She notes the book has received a Reuters review and was listed in Scientific American's new releases. She apologizes in advance for the upcoming promotional blitz, acknowledging that self-promotion isn't her usual style but is necessary for book launches.
key_points: The physical copy of 'Weapons of Math Destruction' has been printed and is available for pre-order
The book has already received media attention including a Reuters BreakingViews review and Scientific American listing
O'Neil is self-aware and somewhat apologetic about the necessary self-promotion authors must do
Her son has volunteered to become a meme to help with book sales
money_quotes: I've been told it's my job for the next few months to be a PR person for my book, so I guess that's what I'm up to.
If you come here for ideas and are turned off by cheerleading, feel free to temporarily hate me, and even unsubscribe to whatever feed I'm in for you!
But please buy my book first.
key_insight: Even authors who write serious critiques of algorithmic power must navigate the awkward transition from intellectual commentator to book promoter.
